perceptual_narrative_logs.visual_narrative_log: (1) The video starts in a restaurant with traditional Chinese lattice decor. Two young Korean men sit at a table while a hand from the first-person perspective points at them. (2) The men look directly at the camera, repeating phrases with somewhat stiff or neutral expressions. (3) A young woman appears on the right side of the frame, showing the men something on a smartphone; one man looks at it and responds with a deadpan face. (4) The woman is seen drinking tea and gesturing about her hair, while the two men respond with perfunctory nods and expressions while sipping from small tea cups.
perceptual_narrative_logs.audio_narrative_log: (1) Upbeat, medium-tempo electronic background music begins. A female voice speaks Chinese clearly, acting as a teacher. (2) Two male voices repeat specific Chinese phrases after the woman, speaking with a slight Korean accent. (3) The female voice transitions into role-playing scenarios, speaking with higher energy. The males respond with short, monotone phrases. (4) Ambient restaurant noise and the sound of tea cups clinking are heard. Laughter from a crowd of off-screen staff occurs during the transitions.
perceptual_narrative_logs.text_narrative_log: (1) "POV 中文學壞的韓國歐巴" - Hook text implying Korean men are learning 'bad' or slang Chinese. (2) "跟隨我唸一遍!" - Functional instruction to repeat. (3) "這些是在台灣很常會說的話" - Functional text explaining these are common phrases in Taiwan. (4) "笑死", "是喔", "真假?" - Large decorative captions highlighting the slang words being taught. (5) "(敷衍)", "(後悔中)" - Decorative emotional stickers describing the men's perfunctory and regretful reactions. (6) Bilingual subtitles in Chinese and Korean appear throughout.

visual_human_presence.countingEvidence: 2 main Korean men, 1 woman on screen, and the hands of the female filmer.
visual_human_presence.mainCharacterDescription: Two young Korean men, one wearing a denim jacket and the other a black and white patterned cardigan. One young woman in a grey hoodie.

visual_scene_and_style.backgroundDescribe: A restaurant with traditional Chinese-style wooden lattice dividers, red lanterns, and other patrons visible in the soft-focus background.

narrative_and_purpose.mainPurpose: The video is designed to entertain by juxtaposing enthusiastic local Taiwanese slang with the deadpan, slightly awkward delivery of Korean men. It functions as a cultural comedy skit that highlights popular linguistic habits in Taiwan through the lens of foreign 'Oppas' being taught to be 'perfunctory' or socially detached in a humorous way.

social_sociology_tags.uniqueSignatures: Combination of Korean identity ('Oppa') with local Taiwanese slang.
social_sociology_tags.freeDescribe: The video capitalizes on the popularity of Korean culture in Taiwan by creating a 'fish-out-of-water' scenario where foreigners learn local nuances.
